title: Surface Delivery
subtitle: A compact starting shape for product surfaces that need design, implementation, verification, and tooling in one place.
scope: A feature or product surface moving from design intent through implementation and verification.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder design, code, ticket, and verification references with real domain sources.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when one product surface needs design intent, implementation state, verification, and tooling to stay visible together.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Treat one product surface as a joined delivery system: intent, implementation state, verification readiness, and tooling should stay visible together without collapsing into a single status.

objective: Make one product surface legible across intent, implementation state, verification readiness, and the tools used to work on it.
successCondition: A reader can identify the main surface, current implementation drift, immediate verification gaps, and which tools are used to evolve the work.

callouts.title: How to use this template
items: Keep the section structure if your domain is primarily about shipping a surface from design to verified behavior.
Replace placeholder references before sharing the rendered HTML as an operational snapshot.

callout.title: Template intent
items: Use this opening section to summarize what exists and where the current delivery pressure is.
Keep it short; the detailed sections below carry the evidence.

data.name: Primary surface
notes: Start with the main surface that should be true when the feature is considered real.
Keep design, code, spec, and tracked interaction references together in one card.

data.name: Fallback and empty state
notes: Include recovery and empty-state surfaces early so they do not disappear behind the main happy path.
Main layout exists, but interaction states and validation affordances still drift from design.
Keep one explicit row for each important drift or missing implementation target.

data.name: Smoke path for primary submit
notes: Add the smallest trustworthy check first, then expand into a richer verification surface when the feature matures.

data.name: Empty state recovery check
notes: Use the checkpoint section when you need a compact list of checks rather than a full evidence-bearing verification model.

data.name: Universal renderer
data.purpose: Render the canonical JSON document into a standalone HTML snapshot with the full compositor embedded.
data.whenToUse: Use whenever you need a shareable artifact or want to refresh an existing rendered living doc.
notes: Use tooling sections for operational instruments, not for source material.
caveats: Rendered HTML is a snapshot and can drift from canonical JSON over time.

data.name: Registry overview generator
data.purpose: Expose the current convergence-type language so contributors can decide whether the doc needs a new section type or just a new section instance.
data.whenToUse: Use when the delivery surface starts needing structure that does not fit the existing type vocabulary.
caveats: Do not invent renderer behavior when a new type belongs in the registry.
